messages.content: Return JSON only. No markdown, no explanation.

What is the exact output of this Python 3 expression?

funcs = []
for i in range(3):
    funcs.append(lambda: i)
[f() for f in funcs]

Return {"values": <list of ints>}.

metadata.mainstream_gap: Classic late-binding closure trap; HumanEval/MBPP rarely test loop-captured lambdas.

title: Trace non-short-circuit bitwise OR with post-increments

metadata.mainstream_gap: Operator evaluation order with side effects; not covered by function-synthesis benchmarks.

title: Decode JSON Pointer segment escapes (~0 and ~1)

messages.content:
RFC 6901 JSON Pointer: within each segment, ~1 means / and ~0 means ~.
Decode the final segment of the pointer /repo~1config~0toggle into its literal string.
Return {"segment": "..."}.

metadata.mainstream_gap: Spec-level escaping detail absent from mainstream coding benchmarks.

title: Python vs C modulo semantics on negative dividends

messages.content:
In Python 3, what is the result of -7 % 3? In C99 the same expression is -1.
Return {"python_mod": <int>, "c99_mod": <int>}.

metadata.mainstream_gap: Language-specific modulo semantics; trivia for synthesis benches but easy to confuse under pressure.

title: Trace disjoint-set connectivity after union sequence

messages.content:
Nodes 0..5 start disconnected. Apply unions in order: (0,1), (2,3), (1,2). After all unions, are nodes 0 and 4 in the same set?
Return {"connected_0_4": <bool>} with lowercase true/false.

metadata.mainstream_gap: Manual DSU trace instead of implementing union-find from spec.

title: Predict Go defer LIFO print order

messages.content:
What does this Go program print to stdout (no spaces/newlines between digits)?

defer fmt.Print(1)
defer fmt.Print(2)
fmt.Print(3)

Return {"output": "..."}.

metadata.mainstream_gap: Defer stack ordering; not represented in Python-centric public benches.

title: Identify first compile error in ownership snippet

metadata.mainstream_gap: Ownership/borrow checker reasoning beyond generic code generation tasks.

title: Reason about asyncio cancellation during await

messages.content:
In asyncio, task A awaits sleep(1). While sleeping, task B cancels A. Which exception does A's coroutine observe when the CancelledError propagates (before Python 3.11's TaskGroup changes)?
Return {"exception": "CancelledError"} exactly.

metadata.mainstream_gap: Async cancellation semantics rarely appear in static function benchmarks.
